WARNING: MacBook Air 13-inch M3 (X2818/MLB). NOT Pro, NOT M4.
description: Boot power-on sequence for M3 MacBook Air. Starts from USB-C cable insertion, ends at display LOGO.

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion - 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V on VBUS.
sequence.what_happens: Port controller passes 5V via DF400 fuse. Output goes to PPDCIN_USBC_AON.
sequence.next_trigger: Port controller detects 5V on VBUS -> begins CC communication

sequence.name: PPCON_ACNSW - Charger Input Active
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. Feeds charger IC.

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up. If battery connected, PPBUS_AON ~12.6V.
sequence.prerequisite: BMU SYS_DETECT circuit (J5150 connector) must enable VBAT output

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON - SVR AON Powers Up
sequence.delay: 3.8V VR enable delayed by D5340 circuit (page 31)
sequence.next_trigger: PP3V8_AON present -> SPMU (U7700) and MPMU (U8100) begin startup

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup - PMU Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and begin generating output rails.

sequence.name: UC300 (5V) & UC710 (3V3) Power Up
sequence.action: Standalone buck converters generate PP5V_S2 and PP3V3_S2 from PPBUS_AON.

sequence.name: SoC AWAKE Rails - DVFS Domains Power Up
M3 Hard Start Complete - SoC Commands Port Controllers
sequence.action: M3 SoC has completed OTP verification. SoC communicates with port controllers.

sequence.name: PD Negotiation - 20V Boost
sequence.action: Port controller uses CC line to negotiate PD 20V from charger.

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Stabilises at Full Power
sequence.action: With 20V on VBUS, PPBUS_AON stabilises at battery voltage with full charging power.

sequence.name: Soft Start - SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: M3 SoC reads SSD, loads iBoot, enables display.
what_happens: SoC reads NAND0 S5E MK2 via PCIe (pages 77-78)
Display power sequencer (UP700/UP710) enables panel rails
San Diego Display PMU generates PP_AVDD, PP_VGH, PP_VGL (page 85)
BEN LP800 drives backlight via PPBUS_AONSW_LCD (page 83)
